RIVER BEND NURSING AND REHABILITATION

CCN: 155621 · EVANSVILLE, IN 47720 · Vanderburgh County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
3400 STOCKER DR, EVANSVILLE, IN 47720
Phone
8124248100
Certified beds
113
Avg daily residents
63 (56% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1997-04-04
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.61 4.09 3.41 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.67 0.72 0.63 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.66 0.91 0.63 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.27 2.47 2.15 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.34 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.09 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.37
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.56
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.18
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
54.5%
annual
RN turnover
33.3%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.45
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.05
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-14 27 19 8 136 1 136
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-09-11 19 13 6 120 2 180
Total weighted health score
147.0
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (55)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
52 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2026-01-30 —
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2026-01-30 —
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. E 2026-01-30 —
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2026-01-30 —
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2026-01-30 —
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-11-25 2025-12-17
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-11-25 2025-12-17
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2025-11-25 2025-12-17
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. E 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. D 2024-11-14 2024-12-13
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-09-10 2024-08-31
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2024-06-05 2024-07-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-05-14 2024-05-30
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-03-01 2024-03-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-07
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2023-09-11 2023-12-07
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. E 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. C 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2023-09-11 2023-12-07
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2023-09-11 2023-11-02
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2023-04-14 2023-05-12
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2021-08-24 2021-09-25
0555 Honor the resident's right to choose his or her attending physician. E 2021-08-24 2021-09-25
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2021-08-24 2021-09-25
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2021-08-24 2021-09-25

Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description

Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag

Deficiencies 0759 — description Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. 0759.description

Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
